Description & Requirements
Job Description

Company Overview

AESC is a global leader in lithium‑ion and LFP battery technology, with a fast-growing footprint across Japan, the UK, France, China, and the USA—soon expanding in Spain with a €1‑billion gigafactory in Extremadura to open by 2026 and create up to 900 direct jobs in phase one. The Spanish facility is designed as the first net‑zero industrial park gigafactory in the country, aligning with AESC’s mission to pioneer sustainable battery manufacturing.


Role Purpose

As a MEP Engineer, you will serve as the Technical Lead for facility MEP systems, taking full responsibility for the end-to-end technical lifecycle management of critical infrastructure, including HVAC, process gases, plumbing and drainage, and electrolyte delivery systems—from design and construction through to operations and maintenance. You will ensure all systems meet the stringent process requirements of lithium-ion battery production (e.g., ultra-low dew point dry rooms, cleanrooms) from an operations-friendly and long-term reliability perspective, enabling safe, reliable, and efficient operations.


Key Responsibilities

During the Project Construction Phase:

Design Review and Technical Assurance
Conduct comprehensive reviews of detailed designs for HVAC, process gases, plumbing and drainage, electrolyte delivery, compressed air, and related systems, focusing on operations convenience, reliability, and total lifecycle cost.
Ensure designs facilitate daily inspections, maintenance, troubleshooting, and equipment replacement, with adequate access space and ergonomic operational interfaces.
Lead technical evaluations for ultra-low dew point environmental control solutions in core areas such as dry rooms and electrolyte filling workshops, cleanroom airflow design, process cooling water systems, specialty gas supply systems, and centralized electrolyte supply and distribution systems.
Provide optimization recommendations for critical equipment selection, piping layout, valve and instrument configuration, balancing initial investment with long-term operational costs.
Tendering and Coordination
Lead or participate deeply in technical coordination for tendering MEP work packages such as HVAC, piping, and electrolyte delivery, defining requirements for equipment reliability, spare parts commonality, and operations and maintenance support in tender documents.
Coordinate interfaces between MEP contractors and with other disciplines to ensure final installation aligns with best practices for operations.
Construction Supervision and Quality Control
Supervise the installation quality and progress of relevant MEP systems, with particular attention to key details impacting future operations such as piping cleanliness, valve operability, instrument visibility, and equipment labeling.
Ensure construction methods and acceptance criteria meet requirements for long-term reliable system operation.
Commissioning and Acceptance Management
Represent the end-user in leading commissioning and acceptance of relevant systems, ensuring performance meets design requirements and that systems are maintainable.
Review commissioning plans and test procedures to verify system reliability and operational convenience.
Witness critical performance tests to ensure stable achievement of system parameters.


During the Project Operation and Maintenance Phase:

System Handover and Standardization
Ensure smooth handover of relevant MEP systems from the project team to the operations team, guaranteeing complete technical documentation and organizing systematic training.
Establish and refine standard operating procedures, maintenance plans, and emergency response protocols based on reliability-centered maintenance strategies.
Daily Operations Management and Technical Support
Be responsible for daily operation management of relevant systems, establishing a key performance indicator monitoring framework.
Lead the team in executing reliability-oriented maintenance activities, analyzing root causes of failures, and implementing corrective and improvement measures.
Provide system operational data support for energy management and process optimization initiatives.
Continuous Improvement and Team Management
Lead system reliability enhancement and maintenance optimization projects based on operational data and maintenance experience.
Train and manage the operations and maintenance technical team, establishing a knowledge management system.
Ensure all maintenance activities comply with relevant regulations and safety standards.


Required Experience & Expertise

Education: Engineering degree or Master's Degree with specializations in HVAC, Chemical Machinery, Process Equipment and Control Engineering, or a related field.
Experience: Minimum 8 years of experience in MEP systems engineering and operations, and desire within high-end manufacturing industries (semiconductors, lithium-ion batteries, biopharmaceuticals, etc.).
Language: Fluency in Spanish (C1 or above) and working proficiency in English (B2 or above).
Core Skills:
Expertise in the design and operations of critical systems such as ultra-low dew point dry rooms, cleanrooms, and process cooling.
Experience in design review or operations of electrolyte or high-purity chemical delivery systems.
Familiarity with key considerations for reviewing designs from an operations perspective and proficiency in maintainability design analysis.
Proficiency with relevant engineering software and maintenance management systems.
Preferred Skills:
Direct experience with electrolyte delivery systems in lithium-ion battery plants.
Familiarity with reliability engineering methodologies (e.g., RCM, FMEA) and CMMS applications.
Proven experience in successfully leading system reliability improvement projects.


Led by a diverse and experienced management team, AESC employs more than 14,000 professionals worldwide. The company combines state-of-the-art technology, Japanese craftsmanship, and a strong track record in safety to deliver advanced battery solutions. AESC offers a comprehensive portfolio across various cathode chemistries and form factors, including LFP, NCM/MCA, and pouch, cylindrical, and prismatic cell formats.

Diamond Foundry Inc. is solving the thermal limitation at the foundation of today's most exciting tech industries - AI & cloud compute, electric-car power electronics, and 5G/6G wireless. We have managed to produce the world's first single-crystal diamond wafers and are now on a mission to put a diamond behind every chip. We are the rare unicorn that has grown fast and profitably. We received $515m in funding and are executing a multi-$B expansion plan for one of the greenest forms of tech manufacturing: converting greenhouse gas into diamond wafers using zero-emission energy.

We are seeking a Facilities Electrical Engineer to lead the electrical infrastructure and critical utility systems of our Single Crystal Diamond (SCD) Ingot Fab. In this high-stakes role, you will manage the full lifecycle of our power architecture—from 132kV Substations and BESS/PV integration to the low-voltage networks supporting ultra-high-purity (UHP) gas and process-cooling systems. You will serve as a key technical lead, bridging the gap among facilities, process engineering, and external contractors to ensure uninterrupted power quality for diamond growth. By driving complex electrical systems installations and optimizing system efficiency, you will safeguard our high-tech manufacturing operations in strict alignment with semiconductor industry standards.
Responsibilities
Plan, manage, and execute electrical facility projects, including factory expansions, equipment installations, and critical utility system upgrades. Oversee high- and low-voltage electrical infrastructure to ensure optimal Fab operations.
Manage contractors and vendors, including bidding, contract negotiations, scope definition, performance tracking, and ensuring work is completed on schedule, within budget, and in accordance with quality standards. Ensure contractors comply with Fab safety protocols, EU directives, and local environmental regulations.
Collaborate with tool install teams to support manufacturing equipment installations and ensure facility readiness.
Utilize AutoCAD or similar software to design facility layouts, piping diagrams, and electrical schematics for facility modifications.
Proficient in ETAP to perform electrical power system modeling and simulation to ensure the safety, reliability, and efficiency of the utility power networks (Modelling, Load Flow & Short Circuit, Protection Coordination, Arc Flash Studies, Technical Reporting).
Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Electrical, Electronics, or Facilities Engineering, or a related field, with 3 to 5 years of experience in electrical project engineering within high-tech manufacturing facilities.
Strong understanding of power electrical distribution systems of high voltage and low voltage.
Experience with construction, expansions, and modifications.
Proven experience in contractor and vendor management, including negotiation, scheduling, compliance enforcement, and performance oversight.
Strong knowledge of building codes, safety regulations, and environmental compliance.

We are looking for skilled, hands-on professionals to strengthen our crane team (40 - 700 tons) as crane operators and drivers. If you enjoy variety and thrive in an active, dynamic environment, this position is for you! As a Crane Operator, you are responsible for independently and safely performing lifting operations.

Together with your colleagues, you will assemble and disassemble your crane in various configurations.

You will manage your crane and its accessories. This includes monitoring its technical condition and performing minor daily maintenance on the crane and associated equipment.

Profile

Analyse the assignment and coordinate with the planner, depot manager, or client where necessary.
Drive the mobile crane to the site via permitted routes.
Comply with general traffic regulations and Sarens-specific safety rules.
Transport materials to and from the site.
Lift materials efficiently and safely.
Conduct route checks.
Step in as a rigger (assist with assembling the cranes and supporting lifting operations).
Perform first-line maintenance of the truck and check all relevant documents.
Assemble and disassemble the building of cranes

Requirements

Minimum of 5 years’ experience working with telescopic or tower cranes.
Proven experience as a crane operator with 40–500+ ton cranes.
Extensive truck-driving experience with a valid license.
Basic knowledge of mechanics and hydraulics.
Holder of a CE driver’s license.
Lifting certificate, load securing certificate, and/or safety certificate (VCA).
Willingness to learn Dutch to communicate effectively with clients.
Strong commitment to safety as the highest priority.
Flexibility regarding tasks, working hours, and locations across Belgium.
A proactive, hands-on attitude with a sense of responsibility and a love of variety.
Preferred: TCVT and safety certificate VCA-VOL.
Valid Passport

What We Offer

Permanent contract
37 hours per week
Relocation support & temporary accommodation
Daily allowance of €10 net per workday
Telephone allowance
Holiday pay and end-of-year bonus
Hospitalisation insurance
Learning and development opportunities
Opportunity to obtain your VCA Certificate at Sarens Academy

Titulo: Enfermera / Enfermero en Los Países Bajos (in situ)
Descripcion_Completa:
Eduployment ofrece grandes oportunidades para profesionales de enfermería en los Países Bajos.

* DEBE ESTAR DISPUESTO/A A TRASLADARSE A LOS PAÍSES BAJOS PARA APROVECHAR ESTA OPORTUNIDAD *

El salario indicado es neto al mes.

EDUPLOYMENT

En Eduployment creemos fervientemente en la idea de que las barreras lingüísticas no deben frenar tu carrera profesional como enfermero/a. Por eso, desde 2002, nos dedicamos a enseñar neerlandés a profesionales de enfermería cualificados de la UE. Pero ofrecemos mucho más que clases de idiomas. También organizamos alojamiento a precios asequibles y proporcionamos formación completa, apoyo práctico y nos ocupamos de todas las tareas administrativas relacionadas con tu traslado. De este modo, podrás concentrarte en lo que mejor sabes hacer: ofrecer unos cuidados de enfermería excelentes.

¿Eres enfermero/a con cualificaciones de la UE? Siga leyendo y descubra lo que podemos ofrecerte.

LOS PAÍSES BAJOS

Los Países Bajos son un país lleno de canales y molinos de viento, famoso por su sistema educativo y su diseño. En los Países Bajos siempre estarás cerca de la naturaleza y de la vida urbana. Tanto si quieres hacer senderismo o montar en bicicleta por los bosques, explorar los cascos antiguos de las ciudades, tomar algo con los amigos, navegar o hacer surf en nuestros famosos ríos y lagos, o relajarte en las dunas y en las costas abiertas, los Países Bajos ofrecen mucho espacio para la exploración y una gran vida social. El país es conocido por su saludable equilibrio entre vida laboral y familiar y su sociedad bien organizada, caracterizada por altos niveles de confianza, bienestar y diversidad cultural.

PROFESIONALES DE ENFERMERÍA

Buscamos diplomados en enfermería dispuestos a desempeñar un papel activo en el cuidado no sólo de la salud física de los pacientes, sino también de su bienestar social, emocional y psicológico en el sector sanitario público neerlandés. Nuestras instituciones sanitarias asociadas te ofrecen un contrato de trabajo directo y a tiempo completo con excelentes paquetes salariales y de beneficios, fantásticas oportunidades para mejorar su CV y un plan de desarrollo personal a medida para impulsar tu carrera hacia nuevas cotas.

TE OFRECEMOS:

Formación en neerlandés (B1/B2).
Asistencia completa para el traslado: alojamiento (vivienda individual), servicio de recogida en el aeropuerto, apertura de cuenta bancaria, solicitud de seguro (de enfermedad), ayuda con el registro BIG (registro nacional neerlandés de asistencia sanitaria) e incluso compensación para el billete de avión (250 EUR como máximo).
Contratos de trabajo directos con la institución sanitaria (semana laboral de 36 horas).
Salario competitivo basado en el convenio colectivo Zorg & Welzijn.
Amplio paquete de prestaciones y compensación adicional por horas extraordinarias y trabajo irregular.
Numerosas oportunidades de especialización.
Un plan de desarrollo personal a medida que incluye formación adicional en idiomas y formación especializada durante el horario laboral, todo pagado por la institución sanitaria.
Acceso a una red nacional y a una plataforma de instituciones sanitarias para progresar en otras disciplinas.
Apoyo, asesoramiento y formación continuos.

THE NETHERLANDS

The Netherlands is a country full of canals and windmills, renowned for its education system and design. In the Netherlands, you will always be close to nature and city life.
